L1.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the session keepalive mechanism of a SCADA/HMI web application. When authentication is enabled, the token renewal logic does not verify that the requesting client holds a valid, authenticated session before issuing a new signed token. Additionally, the token generation routine derives identity and privilege claims from an attacker-controllable HTTP header rather than from the server-side authenticated session state. This allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to obtain an administrator-level JWT and gain full administrative access, enabling arbitrary code execution through built-in scripting and automation features.

ground_truth.reason: The token refresh endpoint mints new JWTs from an attacker-controlled HTTP header without verifying the caller is authenticated, allowing unauthenticated attackers to forge administrator tokens and achieve remote code execution.
